volumeTitle: Antigone. The Women of Trachis. Philoctetes. Oedipus at Colonus
label: Philoctetes (Section 642 - Section 780)

sections.label: Section 642
sections.original: οὔκ , ἀλλὰ κἀκείνοισι ταῦτʼ ἐναντία .
sections.translation: Not so ; this weather is against them also .

sections.label: Section 643
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστι λῃσταῖς πνεῦμʼ ἐναντιούμενον ,
sections.translation: No wind stands in the way of pirates who sense a chance to steal and plunder by force .

sections.label: Section 645
sections.original: ἀλλʼ εἰ δοκεῖ , χωρῶμεν , ἔνδοθεν λαβὼν
sections.translation: Well , if you are so resolved , let us go , once you have taken from the cave whatever you need or desire most .

sections.label: Section 647
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἔστιν ὧν δεῖ , καίπερ οὐ πολλῶν ἄπο .
sections.translation: Yes , there are some things that I need , though the choice is not large .

sections.label: Section 648
sections.original: τί τοῦθʼ ὃ μὴ νεώς γε τῆς ἐμῆς ἔπι ;
sections.translation: What is there that will not be available on board my ship ?

sections.label: Section 649
sections.original: φύλλον τί μοι πάρεστιν , ᾧ μάλιστʼ ἀεὶ
sections.translation: I have a store of a certain herb , whereby I can always

sections.label: Section 650
sections.original: κοιμῶ τόδʼ ἕλκος , ὥστε πραΰνειν πάνυ .
sections.translation: best lull this wound , until it is wholly tamed .

sections.label: Section 651
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἔκφερʼ αὐτό . τί γὰρ ἔτʼ ἄλλʼ ἐρᾷς λαβεῖν ;
sections.translation: Fetch it , then . Now , what else do you wish to take ?

sections.label: Section 652
sections.original: εἴ μοί τι τόξων τῶνδʼ ἀπημελημένον
sections.translation: Any of these arrows that may have been forgotten , and may have slipped away from me , so that I not leave it for another to take .

sections.label: Section 654
sections.original: ἦ ταῦτα γὰρ τὰ κλεινὰ τόξʼ ἃ νῦν ἔχεις ;
sections.translation: Is that indeed the famous bow which you hold ?

sections.label: Section 655
sections.original: ταῦτʼ , οὐ γὰρ ἄλλʼ ἔστʼ , ἀλλʼ ἃ βαστάζω χεροῖν .
sections.translation: This , and no other , that I carry in my hand .

sections.label: Section 656
sections.original: ἆρʼ ἔστιν ὥστε κἀγγύθεν θέαν λαβεῖν
sections.translation: Is there any way that I might have a closer view of it — and handle it , and salute it as divine ?

sections.label: Section 658
sections.original: σοί γʼ , ὦ τέκνον , καὶ τοῦτο κἄλλο τῶν ἐμῶν
sections.translation: To you alone , my son , this shall be granted , along with anything else in my power that is in your interest .

sections.label: Section 660
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἐρῶ γε , τὸν δʼ ἔρωθʼ οὕτως ἔχω ·
sections.translation: I do indeed crave to touch it , but my craving takes this form : if it is lawful , I would be glad . If not , think no more of it .

sections.label: Section 662
sections.original: ὅσιά τε φωνεῖς ἔστι τʼ , ὦ τέκνον , θέμις ,
sections.translation: Your words are reverent , son , and your wish is lawful . For you alone have given to my eyes the light of life and the hope of seeing the land of Oeta , of seeing

sections.label: Section 665
sections.original: ὃς πατέρα πρέσβυν , ὃς φίλους , ὃς τῶν ἐμῶν
sections.translation: my aged father and my friends ; and you alone , when I lay beneath the feet of my enemies , have lifted me beyond their reach . Be bold . The bow shall be yours to handle and to return to the hand that gave it , and you will be able to boast aloud that in reward for your goodness , you alone of mortals have touched it .

sections.label: Section 670
sections.original: εὐεργετῶν γὰρ καὐτὸς αὔτʼ ἐκτησάμην .
sections.translation: Yes , it was by a good deed that I myself won it .

sections.label: Section 671
sections.original: οὐκ ἄχθομαί σʼ ἰδών τε καὶ λαβὼν φίλον ·
sections.translation: I am not sorry that I found you and have gained your friendship , since whoever knows how to render benefit for a benefit received must prove a friend more valuable than any possession . Please , do go inside .

sections.label: Section 675
sections.original: νοσοῦν ποθεῖ σε ξυμπαραστάτην λαβεῖν .
sections.translation: My sickness craves the comfort of your presence . Philoctetes and Neoptolemus enter the cave .

sections.label: Section 1.676
sections.original: λόγῳ μὲν ἐξήκουσʼ , ὄπωπα δʼ οὐ μάλα ,
sections.translation: I have heard a rumor , but never seen with my eyes , how the man who once approached the bed of Zeus was bound upon a

sections.label: Section 1.680
sections.original: κατὰ δρομάδʼ ἄμπυκα δέσμιον ὡς ἔβαλεν παγκρατὴς Κρόνου παῖς ·
sections.translation: swift wheel by the almighty son of Cronus . But of no other mortal do I know , either by hearsay or by sight , that has encountered a doom so repugnant as this of Philoctetes . For though he had wronged no one by force or thievery ,

sections.label: Section 1.685
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἴσος ὢν ἴσοις ἀνήρ ,
sections.translation: but conducted himself fairly towards the fair , he was left to perish so undeservedly . I truly marvel how — how in the world — as he listened in solitude to the breakers rushing around him ,

sections.label: Section 1.690
sections.original: πῶς ἄρα πανδάκρυτον οὕτω βιοτὰν κατέσχεν ·
sections.translation: he kept his hold upon a life so full of grief .

sections.label: Section 1.691
sections.original: ἵνʼ αὐτὸς ἦν πρόσουρος , οὐκ ἔχων βάσιν ,
sections.translation: Here , he alone was his own neighbor , powerless to walk , with no one in the land to be his companion while he suffered — no one to whom he could cry out a lament that would be answered

sections.label: Section 1.700
sections.original: φορβάδος ἐκ γαίας ἑλών ·
sections.translation: envenomed foot , whenever the torment attacked him . Instead he would then creep this way or that , stumbling like a child without his kind nurse , to any place from where his needs

sections.label: Section 1.705
sections.original: χοι πόρου , ἁνίκʼ ἐξανείη δακέθυμος ἄτα ·
sections.translation: might be supplied , whenever the devouring anguish withdrew .

sections.label: Section 2.707
sections.original: οὐ φορβὰν ἱερᾶς γᾶς σπόρον , οὐκ ἄλλων
sections.translation: For food he did not gather the fruit of holy Earth , nor anything else that we mortals feed on by our labor ,

sections.label: Section 2.710
sections.original: πλὴν ἐξ ὠκυβόλων εἴ ποτε τόξων
sections.translation: except when on occasion he obtained food to ease his hunger by means of feathered shafts from his swift-striking bow . Ah , joyless was his life , who for ten years never knew the delight of wine ,

sections.label: Section 2.718
sections.original: νῦν δʼ ἀνδρῶν ἀγαθῶν παιδὸς ὑπαντήσας
sections.translation: But now , after those troubles , he will attain happiness and heartiness in the end

sections.label: Section 2.720
sections.original: εὐδαίμων ἀνύσει καὶ μέγας ἐκ κείνων ·
sections.translation: because of his meeting with this son of a noble race , who after the fullness of many months carries him aboard our sea-crossing hull to his ancestral home ,

sections.label: Section 2.725
sections.original: Μαλιάδων νυμφᾶν
sections.translation: the haunt of Malian nymphs , and to the banks of the Spercheius in that very land where , above Oeta ’ s heights , the hero of the brazen shield approached the gods , illuminated by his father ’ s divine fire .

sections.label: Section 730
sections.original: ἕρπʼ , εἰ θέλεις . τί δή ποθʼ ὧδʼ ἐξ οὐδενὸς
sections.translation: Please , come on . Why so silent with no apparent cause ? And why are you paralyzed ?

sections.label: Section 731
sections.original: λόγου σιωπᾷς κἀπόπληκτος ὧδʼ ἔχει ;
sections.translation: Ai , ai !

sections.label: Section 732
sections.original: ἀᾶ , ἀᾶ .
sections.translation: What is the matter ?

sections.label: Section 733
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν ;
sections.translation: Nothing serious — go on , son .

sections.label: Section 734
sections.original: μῶν ἄλγος ἴσχεις τῆς παρεστώσης νόσου ;
sections.translation: Are you in pain from the disease that frequents you ?

sections.label: Section 735
sections.original: οὐ δῆτʼ ἔγωγʼ , ἀλλʼ ἄρτι κουφίζειν δοκῶ . ὦ θεοί .
sections.translation: No , indeed no . I think it is better now .— Gods , oh !

sections.label: Section 737
sections.original: σωτῆρας αὐτοὺς ἠπίους θʼ ἡμῖν μολεῖν . ἀᾶ , ἀᾶ .
sections.translation: Why do you groan like this and call on the gods ?

sections.label: Section 740
sections.original: τί ποτε πέπονθας ; οὐκ ἐρεῖς , ἀλλʼ ὧδʼ ἔσει
sections.translation: What troubles you ? Speak , do not keep so silent . It is plain enough that you are suffering somehow .

sections.label: Section 742
sections.original: ἀπόλωλα , τέκνον , κοὐ δυνήσομαι κακὸν
sections.translation: I am destroyed , boy — I can never conceal my suffering when you are close . Ah ! Ah ! It shoots through me , shoots straight through ! Oh , the pain , the misery !

sections.label: Section 745
sections.original: ἀπόλωλα , τέκνον · βρύκομαι , τέκνον · παπαῖ ,
sections.translation: I am destroyed , boy — I am devoured ! Ah , by the gods I beg you , if you have a sword ready to hand , strike at my ankle — cut it off now ! Do not spare my life !

sections.label: Section 751
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν οὕτω νεοχμὸν ἐξαίφνης , ὅτου
sections.translation: What new thing has come on you so suddenly that you wail for yourself with these loud shrieks ?

sections.label: Section 753
sections.original: οἶσθʼ , ὦ τέκνον ;
sections.translation: You know , son .

sections.label: Section 753a
sections.original: τί δʼ ἔστιν ;
sections.translation: What is it ?

sections.label: Section 753b
sections.original: οἶσθʼ , ὦ παῖ ;
sections.translation: You know , boy .

sections.label: Section 753c
sections.original: τί σοί ;
sections.translation: What ails you ? I do not know .

sections.label: Section 754a
sections.original: πῶς οὐκ οἶσθα ; παππαπαππαπαῖ .
sections.translation: How could you not know ? Oh , oh !

sections.label: Section 755
sections.original: δεινόν γε τοὐπίσαγμα τοῦ νοσήματος .
sections.translation: Yes , terrible is the burden of your disease .

sections.label: Section 756
sections.original: δεινὸν γὰρ οὐδὲ ῥητόν · ἀλλʼ οἴκτιρέ με .
sections.translation: Terrible beyond telling ! Oh , pity me !

sections.label: Section 757
sections.original: τί δῆτα δράσω ;
sections.translation: What shall I do ?

sections.label: Section 757a
sections.original: μή με ταρβήσας προδῷς ·
sections.translation: Do not betray me because of fear . This plague comes only now and then ,— perhaps when she has been sated with her roamings elsewhere .

sections.label: Section 759a
sections.original: ἰὼ ἰὼ δύστηνε σύ ,
sections.translation: Ah , poor wretch ! Poor man , truly for all your sufferings ! Shall I support you , or somehow offer a helping hand ?

sections.label: Section 763
sections.original: τάδʼ , ὥσπερ ᾐτοῦ μʼ ἀρτίως , ἕως ἀνῇ
sections.translation: No , no . But take this bow of mine — as you earlier asked of me — and keep it in your care and safe

sections.label: Section 765
sections.original: τὸ πῆμα τοῦτο τῆς νόσου τὸ νῦν παρόν ,
sections.translation: until this present bout with my disease is past . For indeed sleep takes me as soon as this pain passes away , nor can it cease before then . But you must allow me to sleep in peace . And if

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: μόλωσʼ ἐκεῖνοι , πρὸς θεῶν ἐφίεμαι
sections.translation: those men come in the meantime , then by the gods I forbid you willingly or unwillingly , or by any skilled trickery , to give up this bow to them , lest you bring destruction at once on yourself and on me , who am your suppliant .

sections.label: Section 774
sections.original: θάρσει προνοίας οὕνεκʼ · οὐ δοθήσεται
sections.translation: Have no fears as to my caution . The bow shall pass

sections.label: Section 775
sections.original: πλὴν σοί τε κἀμοί · ξὺν τύχῃ δὲ πρόσφερε .
sections.translation: into no hands but yours and mine . Give it to me , and may good luck accompany it !

sections.label: Section 776
sections.original: ἰδοὺ δέχου , παῖ · τὸν φθόνον δὲ πρόσκυσον
sections.translation: There , take it boy . And humble yourself before the jealous gods , so that the bow may not prove baneful for you , as it did for me and for him who owned it before me .

sections.label: Section 779
sections.original: ὦ θεοί , γένοιτο ταῦτα νῷν · γένοιτο δὲ
sections.translation: O gods , grant this to the two of us ! And grant us a voyage

sections.label: Section 780
sections.original: πλοῦς οὔριός τε κεὐσταλὴς ὅποι ποτὲ
sections.translation: prosperous and unimpeded , to whatever goal the god may deem right and that our mission provides !
